case_title: MIGUEL VALERA GONZALEZ VS. MICHAEL VANHERWAARDEN ET AL

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF AARON HANCOCK IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO COMPEL (TRANSACTION ID # 76823590) FILED BY DEFENDANT VANHERWAARDEN, MICHAEL , AN INDIVIDUAL VANHERWAARDEN, STEPHANIE , AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE SEP-25-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS MICHAEL VANHERWAARDEN, AND STEPHANIE VANHERWAARDEN'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE UNOPPOSED MOTION BY DEFENDANTS MICHAEL AND STEPHANIE VANHERWAARDEN TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF MIGUEL VALERA GONZALEZ'S RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, IS GRANTED (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-21-2026 Set For Case Management Conference On Mar-11-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanction
Off Calendar Dec-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MICHAEL VANHERWAARDEN AND STEPHANIE VANHERWAARDEN'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTION

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion for terminating sanctions is granted. Plaintiff Miguel Valera Gonzalez has not complied with the court's prior order to respond to form interrogatories and has not shown good cause. Trial is set for January 26, 2026 and the court concludes that defendant will be prejudiced by being forced to go to trial without basic discovery of plaintiff's claims. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-17-2025 Continued To Jan-26-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Sep-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS MICHAEL VANHERWAARDEN, AND STEPHANIE VANHERWAARDEN's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion by defendants Michael and Stephanie Vanherwaarden to compel plaintiff Miguel Valera Gonzalez's responses to form interrogatories, set one, is granted.

Plaintiff is ordered to provide responses to the form interrogatories attached as Exhibit A to the Hancock Declaration within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. Defendants do not seek sanctions, and the court does not award them, finding mitigating circumstances in the withdrawal of Plaintiff's counsel.

The court notes that the motion was originally on for hearing on September 12, 2025, and the court continued it for receipt of proof of service of the motion on Plaintiff. The court has now received a declaration affirming that Plaintiff was notified of the motion and of the continuance. No opposition was received.

Defendants are ordered to serve a copy of the form interrogatories attached as Exhibit A to the Hancock Declaration on Plaintiff with service of the notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Sep-12-2025 Continued To Sep-25-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT MICHAEL VANHERWAARDEN, AND STEPHANIE VANHERWAARDEN's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' unopposed motion shall be continued for proof of service of the motion on Plaintiff.

Per the Declaration of Aaron Hancock, paragraph 5, "Plaintiff's telephone number and email were not provided with the motion to withdraw [filed by Plaintiff's former attorney] and are not on record with the court." The proof of service attached to Defendants' motion states, apparently in error, that all documents were served on Plaintiff exclusively by electronic service. Plaintiff's email address is not stated on the proof of service.

The matter is continued for hearing to September 25, 2025, at 9:00 A.M. in department 301.
